reviews.review_text: I loved it when Blazkowitz went "It's Blazkowitzing time" and blazkowitzed all over the place, 10/10 game 👍

Wolfenstein: The New Order — Achievement Hunter Review

🕒 Time played: 20 hours
🏆 Completion: 100% game completion + all Steam achievements

As an achievement hunter, I completed Wolfenstein: The New Order 100% in 20 hours, unlocking every Steam achievement.

Overall, I really enjoyed the game. As a shooter, it feels great — the gunplay is satisfying, the action is intense, and the gameplay stays fun throughout the entire experience. The story was also surprisingly good and kept me interested from beginning to end.

For my 100% playthrough, I started collecting all the letters, gold items, and Enigma codes right from the first chapter. If you plan on completing the game fully, I highly recommend doing the collectibles as you progress instead of leaving everything for the end.

Another important part of the achievement list is the perk system. There are achievements tied to getting kills in different ways, such as specific weapon kills, stealth kills, and other combat-related challenges. My advice is simple:
if you find a good farming spot, use the checkpoint system and start grinding there. The progress saves, so you can easily farm perks within a single chapter without replaying the whole game repeatedly.

I also highly recommend saving the UBER difficulty achievement for your final playthrough. If you complete the last chapter on UBER difficulty, after the credits you’ll unlock all lower difficulty achievements as well, which saves a lot of time.

From an achievement hunter’s perspective, the game is very manageable and not overly difficult to complete at 100%.

✅ Pros:

Excellent gunplay and combat
Strong story and atmosphere
Fun perk and progression system
Reasonable achievement difficulty
Collectibles are easy to track if done early

❌ Cons:

Some perk grinding required
Collectibles can be missed easily
Replay needed for cleanup if not planned properly

📝 Final Thoughts:

Overall, I had a great time with Wolfenstein: The New Order. The combination of solid shooting mechanics and a strong story made the 100% journey enjoyable rather than frustrating.

For achievement hunters, I definitely recommend:

collecting everything from the start
farming perks through checkpoints
leaving the UBER difficulty run for the end

The game is not too difficult for 100% completion, and even regular players should enjoy the experience.

I hope this review helped you in some way, and I wish everyone good luck on their journey to 100% completion 🏆🔫🙂

This game was actually so fun, I enjoyed it thoroughly, HOWEVER... SPOILER WARNING, there is a plot line that to me felt like it looped itself back into antisemitism. 

[spoiler] How is no one talking about the Da'at Yichud... the secret Jewish society with super-technology??? They literally had such powerful technology, like literally comparable to nukes. is that not pretty weird? it's like giving the Nazis a reason?? [/spoiler]  There's a million other issues with this but I would've loved this game so much more if not for that weird ass plot line.

Wolfenstein: The New Order turned out much better than I expected. I went in with pretty average expectations, but both the story and the gameplay pulled me in a lot more than I thought they would.

The atmosphere is great and the whole game feels surprisingly immersive. I loved the level design because it constantly shows you new things and pushes you to use combat areas in different ways. The weapons also feel really good: heavy, powerful, and properly satisfying when they hit. That “yeah, that landed” feeling is very much there.

The enemy variety is also strong, and you actually need to adjust your strategy depending on what the game throws at you. Because of that, the combat keeps encouraging different approaches instead of becoming too flat. The characters are good too, and the dialogue has a more realistic, mature tone than I expected.

Overall, I had a really fun time with it. It surprised me in the best way: strong atmosphere, satisfying combat, good characters, and much better writing than I was expecting.

8/10 - A very solid game.

What a fantastic game! I just loved it and honestly I regret not playing it sooner. First of all, killing N*zis is hell fun and the game give lots of opportunities to do so. Story is great. Characters are great (Let's go Team Fergus!!). Unexpected love scenes are great. Everything about game is absolute blast. 
I truly enjoyed almost everything about the game. With a few tweaks and some minor improvements, I think it could have been one of the best FPS games ever made. As it is, it is still a fantastic experience and I would happily recommend everyone.

A worthy successor for my favorite game of all time, Return To Castle Wolfenstein. The combat mechanics are fun and crisp, levels are entertaining, lots of secrets, lots of zombies, lots of fun. Totally recommend.

This has to be, by far, one of the best Wolfenstein games ever made. Honestly, I find it pretty upsetting now to see how the later games have turned out, but I digress. The combat, story, graphics, and just everything about the game in general made this one an instant classic. 12 years later, and the game still looks fantastic. Never had a single noticeable bug or glitch to the game either. 9/10
the story is fantastic, gunplay is insanely satisfying and what’s better than taking down nazis. 10/10 experience and tons of replay value
Got the entire series for pennies on the dollar and playing them in order. This game is great and the campaign is pretty girthy too. Great gun mechanics.

this is really late for a review, but this was released before the 2017 downfall of AAA

tl;dr you honestly should experience it even if it's not replayable, it still is a AAA game of the era so you have some cringe here and there but nothing experience breaking like today's slop, the game gives you a reason to play it through it's story and somehow past 2017 high budget games pretty much lost that completly with only a few games here and there that manage to pass the minimum digestability bar like Space Marine 2 (very good game, but like wolfenstein the new order don't expect to put more than a dozen hours into it).

Overall, it is a good game. Comparing it to other titles of the same genre, the graphics were amazing, there were no bugs encountered during my playthroughs, and the story was generally well done, albeit lacking in depth. With specific praise, the female cast wasn't simply sexualized or given one dimensional traits, especially with the secondary villain of the entire story being a woman, and they all actually contributed to the narrative in meaningful ways instead of being eye candy or mothers, as was the case in Metro. [spoiler]Although, the title did not escape unnecessary sex scenes and somewhat lewd perspectives, but I can forgive this as the writers did not make it the end all be all to the women, solely the love interest.[/spoiler] However, that is not to say that all the characterization was good. The protagonist himself was very, very bland in my opinion. Guy who whispers while he kills Nazis about his thought process in an edgy way. It is hard to describe because while I do feel it fits the narrative to have a character like this, it all just kind of came off as cliche. The main love interest and their connection also was just so poorly done in my opinion. It didn't come off as sexist nor typical, but just kind of bland. In this regard, a lot of it just fell short for me. It feels like its something that could have been a bit more meaningful had it had just a touch more polish to making the world feel realistic. Too much of the story just felt like they were hand-holding you through how they intend for you to interpret things. If the writers trusted the audience a little more and perhaps developed the side characters more than just how they were impacted by Nazis, I feel it could have been truly exceptional, but the entirety of the story revolved around the conflict, making it feel less realistic even in scenes where it was supposed to take a backseat. It seems to me though, that with the sensitive nature of a lot of political climates and discussion, perhaps they did not want to make anything humanized and took the more "tell, don't show" approach as to not allow any of the work to be misconstrued as supportive. As for the gameplay, which I felt was secondary to the story in most regards, it was pretty good. They varied it a bit so it didn't get too repetitive but as with all titles such as these, it did often boil down to presenting you with rooms of a few different types of enemies and giving you some guns to let you figure out the rest. The gore was good though, in both the active playing and the cut scenes. They did not shy away from showing off the depravity and inhumanity of war. The ending was meh and felt very unfulfilling to me, but a good and tough boss fight made up for it. Its good. Its not exceptional, but it doesn't have to be. Recommended if on sale.

This one is top-tier for me. It takes a very old-school idea — one angry man with guns against an entire nightmare regime — and somehow turns it into a shooter with real weight, style, and heart.

The gunplay is excellent. Heavy, loud, fast, and satisfying in that classic FPS way where the game doesn’t need to overcomplicate things. You can sneak when you want, go loud when the plan stops being cute, dual-wield like a man solving diplomacy with ammunition, and just push through some really well-paced levels.

What surprised me most is how much I actually cared about the story. B.J. could have been just a walking jawline with weapons, but the game gives him a tired, human side that really works. The alternate-history world is dark and absurd, but it has enough detail and emotion to feel bigger than just “shoot the bad guys in cool locations.”

It’s brutal, cinematic, weirdly heartfelt, and just fun as hell to play. One of the best modern FPS campaigns, and still one of my favorite examples of how to revive a classic without sanding off everything that made it special.

Not usually an arcade FPS kinda guy however this game does and great job balancing to all play styles with it's perk system far better than its sequel in my opinion.

the world building is fantastic and being my first proper introduction to the series beyond Wolfenstein 3d I can say I'm a fan. Also the soundtrack is f***in peak. if you have been curious about the series this is the best place to start. BUY IT NOW!

Gameplay wise The New Order is brilliantly designed. Its mechanics work together seamlessly to support a wide variety of playstyles, you can approach quietly or tactfully just as well as you can guns blazing. I'd also like to highlight the perk system for accommodating the game's versatility: by completing unique challenges you can unlock various upgrades, said upgrades not only serve to further entrench the depth of the gameplay, but also provide concrete motivation to experiment with every mechanic the game has to offer.
   
It also boasts a surprisingly thorough and engaging narrative. The primary antagonists are two of the most compelling I've ever seen in a video game, their cutscenes provide such a harrowing tension, and their actions really lead one to despise them more than any of the other Nazi bastards. Not a feeling every developer can induce.

If you are a fan of FPS games, it is a must play. if you enjoy a compelling narrative, there's a delicious cherry on top. If you have a burning desire to shoot Nazis in the face, there is no other game quite like it.

Saving Fergus is better, fight me, ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ love that champ.

Some technical limitations--it's hardcapped at 60 fps & sometimes the game chooses to open on the wrong monitor--but overall still holds up. Gunplay is punchy and responsive. Cutscenes, worldbuilding and story are all well-crafted.

Oh and don't enter the vents in the Berlin Catacombs (Chapter 10), Blazkowicz [b]will[/b] get stuck.

The game is alright, but I wouldn't say there is anything remarkable about it besides the story, which is fairly good for a shooter. My main issue with Wofenstein: New Order is its awful technical side:

1) The game is locked to 60 FPS, which, unsurprisingly, feels terrible for anyone used to playing shooters at a high frame rate on MnK. There is a mod called id5 Tweaker that can remove the cap; however, not without side effects. It causes heavy stuttering multiple times during every cutscene, occasional issues with sound, and there are a couple of sections where you have to disable it to be able to progress. Nonetheless, I would still recommend using it over playing at 60 FPS.

2) There are frequent texture pop-ins. To fix the problem, you have to use id5 Tweaker to manually set the maximum number of pages per frame and the amount of RAM and VRAM allocated to the cache.

3) The film grain filter is very aggressive and cannot be turned off through the in-game settings. Once again, I had to resort to id5 Tweaker, capable of disabling it, but unfortunately only along with all post-processing.

4) Even with the game installed on an SSD, the loading screens after deaths are pretty long.

5) There are no basic quality-of-life features like toggle ADS and auto-looting. The latter is especially frustrating considering the insane number of items you have to pick up throughout the game, constantly disrupting the pacing.

6) It's not possible to use the original voice-over with subtitles in a different language. Had to mess with the game files to change the subtitle language separately.

I spent far too much time troubleshooting and trying to make this mess function properly.

Another gripe I have with The New Order is the armored enemies. There are only a handful of effective strategies against them, so most of the "challenging" encounters and bosses on the highest difficulty devolve into LaserKraftWerk spam. It is an incredibly dull and repetitive gameplay loop: peek out of cover for a shot or two, run to a charging station, recharge the weapon, and repeat the process as many times as necessary.

Overall, I can’t say I enjoyed my time with this entry in the modern Wolfenstein franchise. Hopefully, the later games are better.
Absolutely amazing FPS game, would even recommend to someone who doesn't/ hasn't played any.

Pure Awesome. it may show its age in some invisible walls and easily fooled enemy ai. But youd be too distracted by all the guts guns and glorious nazi  slaughter to really notice it. 9.5/10 pure awesome!
Good gameplay but old graphics, prepare if you played any AAA titles in the last 5 years to aliasing and bad lighthing and textures

I definitely recommend buying and playing this game. It has a solid feeling like you're playing doom at some point with dual wielding weapons, but this is a bethesda game and it did not dissapoint. Since this is also the beginning of the reboot of the series graphically it's very improved, the story did catch my attention here and there, but I guess the historical exaggeration of the "what if scenario" is a bit too much to me, seeing all the scenes in prison camps and overhearing conversations etc. Uber difficulty was not too hard once I've gotten used to the drops and the weapons, only died a lot of times when I was infiltrating Deathshead's compound since there is an enemy on every corner. Collectables were also a nice catch, the perk system, cracking the enigma code, a variety of different difficulties,
all in all I would recommend and give it a great grade!

The game was great. I enjoyed every aspect of it. My only problem was in the final boss because the game doesn't tell you how to take Deathshead's forcefield off to get to phase 2 where you actually fight him so it took me a while to realise than you have to get to the cannons and shoot the zepelins. Also i dont know how anyone can figure out by themselves that you have to throw an emp grenade after that to trigger the next phase. Besides that,the game was awesome. Might replay it in the figure to get the alternate storyline by saving the other dude in the prologue instead of Fergus

genuinely some of the most superb worldbuilding in a game, as well as a fairly emotional story to boot.

also killing nazis is fun :)
Finished the entirety of the game in 24 hours around. Greatest game ever, will have u addicted and playing till 2 am. Even the main character is lovable, and a great person. Thank you!
Wolfenstein franchise revived full force; an absolute masterpiece of a game. Highly recommend it to anyone who likes fps games.

Even though Wolfenstein: The New Order is an old game by today’s standards, I was genuinely surprised by how insanely good the graphics still look. The atmosphere, gunplay, and especially the story completely hooked me, even though I played it for the first time in 2026. Definitely one of those games that aged like fine wine.

I was much too harsh on this game when it released. I had a very narrow idea of what Wolfenstein should be and how our main protagonist should act.

12 years later: New Order is just a fun joyride that knows when to take itself serious and when to lean into the pure absurdity of the world its created that makes for some genuinely funny moments.

The graphics are great. Then it needed the "hotwire" skill where you have to manipulate wire with your mouse, quickly.
I was not  able to do this this. Since it was a game blocker, I uninstalled the game.

Wolfenstein was a game about shooting Nazis. This game is about finding folders and moldy concrete. 5 minutes of shooter gameplay followed by ten hours of movie and fetch quests. It is terrible. If I had known it was a Bethesda game I would have never bought it.

This game's bad.

I foolishly thought that since it was originally an ID software's IP, that it would be up to these standards.

+ Not much on the positive. I'll be nice and say that at least the missions themes are varied and sometime original

- The gameplay/gunplay is as basic as it get. No depth or technicity! It's not rewarding to play, feels bland and it shows that the devs have no idea what they were doing
- I played on Death Incarnate difficulty (2nd to hardest difficulty). It was horribly balanced. The game was clearly balanced around a lower difficulty (probably the normal one) and then they pumped up numbers without any thought for higher ones. I hope you like damage sponges  who can kill you in 2 seconds at close range. Again incompetent design.
- Last boss is garbage tier and really awful to fight on these higher difficulties.
- I was impressed by how bad the performance were. My current PC is still pretty strong by 2026 standards and was a brute 3 years ago. That a 2014 game can make it flinch (in 4k but still) tells volume about the game's optimisation.
- I didn't play for the story or for some profound content but really the game would have been better with just a few lines here and there to set up the mood. It's cringe like something on the level of a grotesque comedy that takes itself seriously. The violence in it is also quite disturbing. Like a sick mind trying to find justification for its sadism.

Rating: 4/10 maybe 3/10

At times this game feels VERY good, but it also has really stupid sections of the game that are boring AF and I have no idea why they exist. World-building? Story? These things can be handled through cut-scenes (and half the time they are), so why do we need the walking simulator sections? 

I'd mind it less if it was just 5 minutes of walking through an interesting environment, but this game takes it way too far. There is a mission (NOT A SIDEQUEST) where you have to literally find and collect toys for a manchild. I got stuck on this and wasted tons of time looking for these stupid toys, while thinking "why the ♥♥♥♥ is this in the game?" It was really irritating and killed the pacing. There are more walking simulator/find & fetch something missions, but that one was the worst. I bought this to shoot nazis, not walk around in circles for "world building". 

The combat sections do feel awesome though, especially when you get to fight big hordes. I'm less fond of some the boss fights, as some of them feel really out of place imo. There is a fight near the end where you are basically fighting a massive nazi Megatron. The scale is cool and all, but gameplay-wise it just felt and looked silly. Destroying the thing doesn't even look like it should be possible, even in a crazy exaggerated fictional universe. This would have been a minor problem, but comined with the walking simulator and time-wasting sections, these kinds of "fights" just further diluted the fun with tedium.

When it plays like a classic shooter, its awesome. But there is too much empty calories in between and they almost exhausted my patience. Wolfenstein Old Blood is much better, because it doesn't waste your time and gives you the good stuff constantly, proving again that a shorter, more concentrated game is usually better. 

I enjoyed the game more often than not, but I will give it a thumbs down, because my focus on the negatives is probably more useful to potential buyers than the praise, of which this game already gets plenty.
worst pc performance ive ever seen on a triple a game bar the dog feces EA passes off as "software"
A well-told story with cathartic violence. Killing Nazis never gets old.

you are always empowered to eliminate the 'very bad people' from an important point in time with absolutely no reservations. more games need this energy today. I'm sad it'd been cooking in my backlog for so long but I had to play it after having fun with the 2nd one on my Switch.
One of the more fun cover shooters, but the shotgunner has too much damn health. I like the story presentation though.

Really fun game about shooting nazis. You should play it.

Wolfenstein gives me a lot of Boss Baby vibes. The story revolves around nothing, it's mostly a vehicle for getting to different places where you get to kill a bunch of nazis. That doesn't mean it isn't pretty well thought out. A lot of thought and research was put into the nazi ideology and how they probably would have gone about reshaping Europe if they won WWII and also had a bunch of super powerful sci-fi technology, as well as the random accurate depiction of a concentration camp that flashbanged me in the middle of my kill a bunch of nazis game. Even though as a whole I don't think the story is anything to write home about, a lot of thought was definitely put into it and I can respect that.

The gameplay is also really cool. Plenty of enemy variety, plenty of different weapons and upgrades to mess around with, different weapons for when you're in the 40's and 60's, and sadly it fumbles the bag later in the game by effectively spamming a bunch of the heavily armored enemies that force you to use almost exclusively laser and explosive weapons. It's tolerable for most of the game, usually just a difficulty spike to test what you know about your arsenal, but eventually it just becomes a slog to repeat the same fight again and again because you exhausted most of your explosives and battery in the two fights before that were also armor spam.

I'd still definitely recommend playing this game, I'd just recommend being better at games than I am.

Honestly, one of the best story-driven shooters ever made. The story is gripping, the characters are amazing, and BJ Blazkowicz is just a badass. 

The gunplay feels incredibly satisfying and heavy, especially when you dual-wield shotguns. It balances stealth and loud action perfectly. 

Even years after release, the graphics and atmosphere still hold up. Highly recommend it if you want a great single-player campaign with an awesome soundtrack. 10/10
